title: behen antelopum
side_effects: Behen antelopum can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, including diarrhea, nausea, and stomach cramps, due to its high toxicity levels. Prolonged consumption of the plant's extracts can lead to liver damage and kidney failure in severe cases. Other potential side effects include headaches, dizziness, and allergic reactions, particularly skin irritation and respiratory issues. If not handled correctly, the plant's sap can also cause skin burns and blisters.
